# W28T1 Implementation Plan

## Task Goal

Establish the shared benchmark infrastructure for all Wave 28 benchmark files. This task creates the canonical size matrix, deterministic data generators, and reusable non-contiguous fixtures used by every later benchmark task.

## Related Design Documents

- `design/27-benchmark.md` §3
- `design/27-benchmark.md` §5.2
- `design/27-benchmark.md` §5.4
- `design/27-benchmark.md` §6.1-§6.3
- `design/27-benchmark.md` §7, Wave 1 T1-T2
- `design/01-architecture.md` §3, `benches/` tree
- `design/00-coding.md` §2.1-§2.7, §3.1-§3.3

## Dependencies

- W21 complete, as required by `build/SUMMARY.md`
- `Cargo.toml` already contains the nine `[[bench]]` entries described in `design/27-benchmark.md` §5.1

## Files to Create/Modify

- `benches/utils/mod.rs`
- `benches/utils/generators.rs`
- `Cargo.toml`, only if the benchmark entries from `design/27-benchmark.md` §5.1 are still missing

## Implementation Details

### Shared constants

Define the exact benchmark size tables from `design/27-benchmark.md` §5.2:

- `SIZES_1D: &[usize] = &[64, 65_536, 16_777_216]`
- `SIZES_2D: &[(usize, usize)] = &[(8, 8), (256, 256), (4096, 4096)]`
- `SIZES_3D: &[(usize, usize, usize)] = &[(4, 4, 4), (64, 32, 32), (256, 256, 256)]`

### Generator functions and fixtures

Implement the exact shared generators documented in `design/27-benchmark.md` §5.2:

- `sequential_1d(n: usize) -> Tensor1<f64>`
- `sequential_2d(rows: usize, cols: usize) -> Tensor2<f64>`
- `StridedFixture1D { owner: Tensor2<f64> }`
- `impl StridedFixture1D { fn view(&self) -> TensorView1<'_, f64> }`
- `strided_view_1d(n: usize) -> StridedFixture1D`

The strided view must come from an F-order 2D owner and a slice-based row selection so later benchmarks can measure non-contiguous penalties without inventing new layouts.

### Measurement support expectations

This task does not add benchmark loops yet, but the utilities must support the methodology in `design/27-benchmark.md` §6.1:

- deterministic input generation
- pre-generation outside timed loops
- reuse across warm-up and measurement phases
- compatibility with `std::hint::black_box` and `std::time::Instant`

## Test Plan

- `cargo bench --bench math -- --list`
- `cargo bench --bench reduction -- --list`
- `cargo bench --bench construction -- --list`
- Verify all later benchmark files can import `utils::{generators, SIZES_1D, SIZES_2D, SIZES_3D}` without redefining constants

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `benches/utils/mod.rs` exports `generators` and the three size tables
- [ ] `benches/utils/generators.rs` provides deterministic sequential 1D and 2D tensor generators
- [ ] A reusable non-contiguous 1D fixture exists and is built from a legal F-order owner plus slicing
- [ ] No random or timed-loop data construction is required by later benchmark files
- [ ] The utilities match `design/27-benchmark.md` §5.2 exactly

## Development Workflow

1. Confirm all benchmark entry points already exist in `Cargo.toml`.
2. Create `benches/utils/mod.rs` with the shared exports and size constants.
3. Implement the sequential and strided generators in `benches/utils/generators.rs`.
4. Compile one or more benchmark targets with `--list` to verify the shared module resolves cleanly.
